title: Chrome and Edge performance memory and loading gate
summary: Measure Chrome/Edge Web product-gate performance, memory and loading against the reviewed M6 Web release criteria.
acceptance: Chrome and Edge evidence covers 30 TPS P95 <= 12 ms target, main-thread P95 <= 12 ms target, memory no-growth and initial compressed download target or documented failure
Evidence uses product-gate scale from the Web release gate: 192 x 192, 40 active actors, 20k entities, lamp network, dossiers/events and 90 TPS requests where implemented
Records whether Web is same-spec, lower fast-forward, lower cap, demo-only or cancellation candidate
Does not weaken existing 10 percent warning or 20 percent blocking benchmark thresholds

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Produce browser performance, memory and loading evidence for the Web tier verdict.
forbiddenPaths: product behavior fixes inside benchmark task
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: local hardware variance affects browser metrics.
Risk: browser tooling cannot capture stable memory metrics.
File ownership is limited to benchmarks, web gate tooling and performance docs.
ownerGate: Block on greater than 20 percent regression without accepted explanation
Block if evidence uses empty-world results
Block if Web tier decision would cancel Web without owner approval
m0M5RegressionCoverage: Run M5 invariant and final hash checks when product behavior changes
Preserve M5 final world/read-model hashes unless reviewed migration accepts new baseline
benchmarkImpact: May add M6 browser gate measurements and artifacts but must not weaken baseline thresholds.
reviewRouting: qa-performance completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies real browser evidence and threshold discipline.
rollbackModel: Revert M6 browser benchmark/tooling changes; keep failed evidence in report if gate blocks.

sparkEligibility.reason: Performance baseline and product gate verdict evidence are not Spark-eligible.
